title: Saudi Crude Exports Primed For Uptick In May
section_path: Saudi Crude Exports Primed For Uptick In May
section_path_text: Saudi Crude Exports Primed For Uptick In May
summary:
Main
Saudi crude exports are primed for growth in May as the Kingdom's production quotas increase, coinciding with more aggressively discounted prices, while initial indications also point to weaker-than-usual uptick in seasonal domestic demand. By Jay Maroo
Saudi Arabia's crude/condensate exports have maintained a relatively tight range (see chart below) since July 2024, underlining a firm adherence to production quotas agreed with OPEC+. However, given the most recently announced 411kbd production unwind in May, Saudi Arabia's production quota will be effectively 160kbd higher than April levels.
